target_fund_identity.format_clue: List of sub-funds with launch dates in the Directors' report, a dedicated column in the Statement of Financial Position indicating the base currency (GBP), and a summary table in the notes.
target_fund_identity.evidence_quote: As of the date of this report, the Company has five active sub-funds, Trojan Fund (Ireland) which launched on 13 February 2012, Trojan Income Fund (Ireland) which launched on 12 April 2013...

fund_size_history.format_clue: A row 'Net assets attributable to holders of redeemable participating shares' at the bottom of the Statement of Financial Position, with a dedicated column for the target fund for years 2025 and 2024.
fund_size_history.evidence_quote: Net assets attributable to holders of redeemable participating shares 563,639,155 (as at 31 Jan 2025) and 582,733,443 (as at 31 Jan 2024).

share_classes_catalog.format_clue: Multiple tables in Notes 14 and 18, clearly sectioned by sub-fund. Note 14 provides number of shares. Note 18 provides total capital per class ('Net asset value') and price per share ('Net asset value per share') for 2025, 2024, and 2023.
share_classes_catalog.evidence_quote: Trojan Fund (Ireland) Class I EUR accumulation ... Closing balance 51,321,618.74 (Note 14). Net asset value ... Class I EUR accumulation EUR 62,809,554 (Note 18). Net asset value per share ... Class I EUR accumulation EUR 1.2238 (Note 18).

fee_structure.format_clue: Narrative text and tables in Notes 6, 7, and 8 detailing Management Company, Annual Management Services, and Depositary fees. A comprehensive 'Total Expense Ratio' table in Appendix 2 provides the TER/OCF for each share class.
fee_structure.evidence_quote: Note 7: Annual management services fee ... Trojan Fund (Ireland) ... All Class O shares 1.00%. Note 6: Management company fee ... up to 0.02% of the net asset value. Appendix 2: Total Expense Ratio ... Trojan Fund (Ireland) Class O GBP accumulation 1.11%.

fund_economics_yearly.format_clue: The Statement of Comprehensive Income contains a dedicated column for 'Trojan Fund (Ireland)' with line items for 'Annual management services fee', 'Total operating expenses', and 'Increase in net assets attributable to holders of redeemable participating shares'.
fund_economics_yearly.evidence_quote: Trojan Fund (Ireland) ... Annual management services fee 5,431,961 ... Total operating expenses 6,131,690 ... Increase in net assets attributable to holders of redeemable participating shares from continuing operations 38,830,249

currency_conversion_rates.format_clue: A table in Note 11 'Exchange rates' lists the spot foreign exchange rates used to convert assets and liabilities to the functional currency of the Company at the reporting date.
currency_conversion_rates.evidence_quote: 11. Exchange rates. The following spot foreign exchange rates were used to convert the assets and liabilities held in foreign currencies other than the functional currency of the Company at the reporting date. 31 January 2025 ... Currency Exchange rate to GBP ... United States Dollar 1.241050

asset_allocation_history.format_clue: The 'Schedule of investments' for the target fund provides a detailed list of holdings with subtotals and percentages for asset classes like Equities, Closed-ended funds, and Debt securities. Cash is on the Statement of Financial Position.
asset_allocation_history.evidence_quote: Trojan Fund (Ireland) ... Total equities (2024: 26.69%) 174,181,246 30.88% ... Total debt securities (2024: 70.88%) 382,450,509 67.85%

geographic_allocation_history.format_clue: The 'Schedule of investments' for the target fund groups holdings by country, providing a clear geographic breakdown for both equities and debt securities.
geographic_allocation_history.evidence_quote: Trojan Fund (Ireland) ... Equities ... France ... Pernod Ricard SA ... 0.71% ... Ireland ... Experian PLC ... 1.11% ... Switzerland ... Chubb Ltd ... 1.12%

portfolio_metrics.format_clue: Narrative text in the Investment Manager's report and a table in the notes. The benchmark is mentioned for comparison. Duration is mentioned in the manager's commentary and quantified in the risk section notes.
portfolio_metrics.evidence_quote: For comparative purposes, the UK Retail Price Index return was +3.7% (p.6). The sub-fund aims to achieve growth in capital, ahead of inflation (UK Retail Prices Index) (p.27). The duration of the portfolios of Trojan Fund (Ireland) ... 31 January 2025 ... 3.50 (p.50).

top_holdings.format_clue: A full 'Schedule of investments' on page 58 lists all holdings with name, value, and % of NAV. The Investment Manager's report on page 6 discusses the largest holdings and strong performers narratively.
top_holdings.evidence_quote: Unilever, the Fund’s largest equity holding, performed well. The stock was up +24.5% for the year to the end of January 2025, contributing +1% to returns. Other strong performers throughout the year included Alphabet, Visa and American Express.

performance_history.format_clue: A narrative summary for the main share class is in the Investment Manager's report. A detailed table in Appendix 2 provides performance percentages for multiple share classes over the last three annual periods.
performance_history.evidence_quote: The Trojan Fund (Ireland) Class O GBP accumulation Shares (the “Fund”) returned +8.8% in the period. For comparative purposes, the UK Retail Price Index return was +3.7%... (p.6). Performance – Trojan Fund (Ireland) ... 31/01/2024 to 31/01/2025 ... Class O GBP accumulation 8.76% (p.77).

manager_qualitative.format_clue: Narrative text in the Investment Manager's report under 'Market Review', 'Portfolio Review', and 'Outlook' sections. The fund's official objective is stated in the notes.
manager_qualitative.evidence_quote: Elevated equity valuations, heightened concerns around the sustainability of US government debt levels and rising geopolitical tensions, are further reasons for the strategy’s conservative stance. ... At an aggregate level, the outlook for prospective equity returns is low...

manager_thesis_and_decisions.format_clue: Narrative text in the 'Portfolio Review' section of the Investment Manager's report, detailing new purchases, additions, sales, and reductions with rationale for each decision.
manager_thesis_and_decisions.evidence_quote: In terms of portfolio activity, the Fund started two new holdings in Verisign and Chubb reflecting our desire to add attractive stock-specific opportunities when they arise. ... We sold the Fund’s holding in Becton Dickinson in June. ... We reduced holdings in American Express and Procter & Gamble on valuation grounds.
